title: Bootstrap Drift & Lint Rules
description: Declarative rule definitions consumed by 'bootstrap drift' and 'bootstrap lint'. Each rule has a jq/JMESPath expression so it can be evaluated from .sh without extra dependencies. Severity controls exit code; enable/disable flags allow per-rule opt-out.

rule_set_version.description: Version of this rule set. Bump when adding, removing, or changing semantics of a rule.

defaults.description: Default severity and enablement for rules that don't override them inline.

severity.description: Default severity if a rule omits its own.

enabled.description: Default enablement flag if a rule omits its own.

fail_on.description: Minimum severity that causes the lint command to exit non-zero.

inputs.description: Default file inputs that rules can reference via {{struct}}, {{state}}, {{generated}} placeholders.

struct_file.description: Path to the project architecture descriptor (the main schema).

state_file.description: Path to the generator-emitted state manifest with hashes and registered outputs.

schema_file.description: Path to the JSON Schema for struct.json.

categories.description: Grouping of rules by concern. Each category has its own severity default.

drift.description: Rules for schema <-> artifact consistency (structural drift detection).

structure.description: Schema structure anti-patterns (cycles, dead code, dangling references).

complexity.description: Complexity thresholds (oversized modules, deep chains, fan-out).

convention.description: Naming and style conventions enforced across modules and components.

Rule.description: A single lint or drift rule. The check describes how to evaluate it; the executor maps that to jq/git/stat calls.

id.description: Stable kebab-case id; used in CLI flags (--disable=id) and machine-readable reports.

description.description: One-sentence explanation of what this rule catches.

severity.description: Severity override for this rule (otherwise inherits from category.default_severity then defaults.severity).

enabled.description: Per-rule enable flag (otherwise inherits from defaults.enabled).

check.description: How the rule is evaluated at runtime.

type.description: Evaluator type: jq/jmespath run against a JSON file, stat checks filesystem, git inspects repo state, schema validates a file.

input.description: Input file path, supporting placeholders: {{struct}}, {{state}}, {{generated}}, {{schema}}. Defaults to struct.json.

expression.description: jq or JMESPath expression whose result is compared against expected using 'compare'.

expected.description: Expected value (type matches expression output: string/number/boolean/array).

compare.description: Comparison operator applied to (expression result, expected). 'matches' treats both as regex (PCRE/ERE).

schema_ref.description: For check.type=schema: path or URI of a JSON Schema to validate input against.

glob.description: For check.type=stat: glob pattern of files to test (e.g. '{{generated}}/**/*.py').

git_ref.description: For check.type=git: a git ref (branch, tag, commit) or HEAD.

remediation.description: Suggested fix, rendered to humans when the rule fires.

docs_url.description: External documentation page explaining the rule in depth.

tags.description: Free-form tags for grouping and filtering (e.g. ['schema', 'breaking']).

rules.description: struct.json content hash does not match the hash recorded in state.json; generated/ is stale relative to the source schema.
rules.remediation: Re-run 'bootstrap.sh --target all' to refresh generated/, or commit the regenerated files so state.json is updated.

fix.description: Re-run bootstrap generate to refresh generated/ and update state.json with the new struct hash.

rules.description: Every output path registered in state.json must exist on disk. Missing files indicate a partial or aborted generation.
rules.remediation: Run 'bootstrap.sh --target all' to regenerate missing outputs, then verify the script exits 0.

fix.description: Re-run bootstrap generate to recreate any missing generated/ outputs.

rules.description: Files under generated/ that are NOT declared in struct.json (directly or transitively) accumulate as orphans and confuse consumers.
rules.remediation: Delete files under generated/ that are not produced by any generator, or update the generator manifest to claim them.

fix.description: Move orphan files (those not declared in state.json) into generated/.orphan-quarantine/<timestamp>/. Or add them to .gitignore via 'echo <path> >> .gitignore'.

rules.description: A component import cycle (A -> B -> A) makes topological sorting impossible and indicates a layered-boundary violation.
rules.remediation: Break the cycle by inverting one of the imports (e.g. introduce an interface module) or merging the two components.

rules.description: A component with no imports AND no inbound callers is either dead code or has not yet been wired into the architecture.

check.expression: [.modules[].components[] | select((.imports // [] | length) == 0) | .name] as $leafless | [.. | objects | select(.imports? != null) | .imports[]?] as $importers | ($leafless - [$importers[]?]) | length

rules.remediation: Either remove the isolated component from struct.json or add it to another component's imports to declare a real dependency.

fix.description: Walk modules[].components[] and drop any component that has empty imports[] and is not referenced by any other component's imports[] (orphan). The removed components are returned as 'removed[]' in the preview so the user can review.

rules.description: A module carrying more than MAX_COMPONENTS components becomes hard to navigate and usually indicates missing sub-modules.
rules.remediation: Split the oversized module into 2-3 cohesive sub-modules grouped by responsibility, then update imports.

fix.description: For modules with >12 components, emit a preview plan that splits them into N sub-modules by grouping components around their imports graph. The split is preview-only because responsibility grouping is a human decision; the auto-fix writes 'generated/.fix-preview/oversized-module-<timestamp>.json' with the suggested split.

rules.description: Longest transitive import chain exceeds MAX_DEPTH, indicating layered coupling that hurts refactorability.
rules.remediation: Flatten the chain by extracting shared interfaces, or move the leaf component closer to the layer that actually consumes it.

rules.description: All component names must be PascalCase (leading uppercase, alphanumerics only) to match the generator's symbol mapping.
rules.remediation: Rename the offending component to PascalCase (e.g. 'tokenizer' -> 'Tokenizer') and update every imports reference.

fix.description: Lowercase first letter -> uppercase first letter for every component name (e.g. 'tokenizer' -> 'Tokenizer'), and propagate the rename across every imports[] / depends_on[] / blocks[] reference in struct.json.

rules.description: Every integration_point must have a peer_path that is a non-empty string starting with '/'.

check.expression: [.integration_points[] | select((.peer_path | type) != "string" or (.peer_path | startswith("/")) | not) | .id] | length

rules.remediation: Provide an absolute peer_path (e.g. '/api/users') on each integration_point in chimeric.json.

rules.description: Every integration_point.peer_method must be a standard HTTP verb (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS).
rules.remediation: Set peer_method on each integration_point to a standard HTTP verb (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, or OPTIONS).

rules.description: Object-valued field mappings must declare a 'type' that is one of the supported scalar/container types (string, integer, number, boolean, array, object).

check.expression: [.integration_points[].field_mapping // {} | to_entries[] | select((.value | type) == "object" and ((.value.type // "") as $t | ($t | IN("string","integer","number","boolean","array","object")) | not)) | .key] | length

rules.remediation: Set 'type' on every object-valued field mapping to one of: string, integer, number, boolean, array, or object.

rules.description: Invariants declared on integration_points must have a non-empty 'expression' string.
rules.remediation: Provide a non-empty 'expression' string on every invariant in integration_points[], or remove the invariant entry.
